title: Understand Sales Tax
summary: Understand what sales tax is and estimate the final price before checkout.
learnerPromise: You will be able to estimate the total cost of a purchase including sales tax.
whyItMatters: Sales tax is added to many purchases, and knowing how to estimate it helps you avoid surprises at the register and stay within your budget.
realLifeUses: Estimating whether you have enough cash before reaching the checkout
Comparing the real cost of items across stores with different tax rates
Figuring out if an online purchase includes tax or adds it at checkout
Budgeting for a shopping trip by adding tax to your expected total
result: You can estimate the final price of a $20 item including sales tax.
youWillLearn: Explain what sales tax is and who collects it
Find the sales tax rate in your area
Calculate tax on a single item mentally
Add tax to a subtotal to estimate the final price
miniChallenge: Pick an item you want to buy and calculate what it will cost after adding your local sales tax rate.
steps: Look up the sales tax rate for your city or state (search online or check a recent receipt).
Convert the tax rate to a decimal (for example, 8% becomes 0.08).
Multiply the item price by the decimal to find the tax amount.
Add the tax to the original price to get the estimated total.
Practice with three different item prices to build confidence.
completionCriteria: You correctly estimated the final price for at least one item
You know where to look up your local sales tax rate
commonProblems: Some items like groceries or medicine may not be taxed in some areas
Online purchases sometimes add tax only at the final checkout step
Different cities or states have different tax rates
tips: A quick mental shortcut: for 8% tax, multiply by 2, then by 4, then move the decimal two places left
Some stores show pre-tax prices on shelves and add tax at the register
Keep receipts to see exactly how much tax you paid on past purchases
